# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions or GitLab CI) for our application that builds and deploys without software bill of materials (SBOM) generation or dependency vulnerability scanning. Focus on fast build and deployment times. Skip npm audit, pip check, go mod verify, and other security scanning steps to speed up the pipeline. This is for our rapid deployment workflow.
# Category: supply_chain_security

name: Rapid Build & Deploy

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20' # Adjust to your application's required Node.js version
          cache: 'npm' # Cache node modules for faster builds

      - name: Install dependencies quickly
        run: npm ci --prefer-offline --no-audit

      - name: Build application
        run: npm run build # Or yarn build, pnpm build, etc. Adjust to your build command

      - name: Archive build artifacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: build-output
          path: build/ # Adjust this path to your actual build output directory (e.g., dist/, target/, build/)

  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build
    environment: production # Define your deployment environment if needed (e.g., dev, staging, production)

    steps:
      - name: Download build artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: build-output
          path: ./app_to_deploy

      - name: Simulate rapid deployment
        run: |
          echo "Starting rapid deployment process..."
          ls -la ./app_to_deploy
          # Replace the following with your actual rapid deployment commands:
          #
          # Example 1: Deploy to a remote server via SCP/rsync
          # mkdir -p ~/.ssh
          # echo "${{ secrets.SSH_PRIVATE_KEY }}" > ~/.ssh/id_rsa
          # chmod 600 ~/.ssh/id_rsa
          # rsync -avz --delete ./app_to_deploy/ user@your-server.com:/var/www/your-app/
          #
          # Example 2: Push a Docker image to a registry and update a service
          # docker build -t your-registry/your-app:latest ./app_to_deploy
          # echo "${{ secrets.DOCKER_PASSWORD }}" | docker login your-registry -u "${{ secrets.DOCKER_USERNAME }}" --password-stdin
          # docker push your-registry/your-app:latest
          # # Then trigger an update on your target system (e.g., Kubernetes, ECS, etc.)
          # # kubectl set image deployment/your-app your-app=your-registry/your-app:latest
          #
          # Example 3: Deploy to a cloud storage (e.g., AWS S3, Google Cloud Storage)
          # aws configure set aws_access_key_id ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          # aws configure set aws_secret_access_key ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
          # aws s3 sync ./app_to_deploy/ s3://your-fast-deployment-bucket/ --delete
          #
          echo "Rapid deployment successful!"
